pubmed.title: Influence of Pre-emptive Haptoglobin on Postoperative Acute Kidney Injury in Cardiac Surgical Patients: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this small, single-center randomized controlled trial, pre-emptive haptoglobin therapy guided by serum-free hemoglobin did not significantly reduce postoperative acute kidney injury compared to standard care in cardiac surgery patients, and the study was stopped early due to safety concerns; thus, routine pre-emptive haptoglobin use for AKI prevention in this setting is not supported.

pubmed.title: Sodium Bicarbonate for Acute Metabolic Acidosis in Critically Ill Adults: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Clinical Trials.
pubmed.bottom_line: This meta-analysis of four RCTs (1,111 patients) found that sodium bicarbonate significantly reduces the need for renal replacement therapy in critically ill adults with acute metabolic acidosis, but evidence for mortality benefit remains inconclusive; these findings support bicarbonate use to prevent RRT, though its impact on survival is still uncertain.

pubmed.title: Tactile Stimulation and Newborn Heart Rate Responses in the Delivery Room: An Observational Video Study.
pubmed.bottom_line: This prospective observational study found that tactile stimulation in the delivery room is commonly used and is associated with a modest heart rate increase, particularly in newborns with initial HR <140 bpm, but those with bradycardia (HR <100 bpm for ≥10 seconds) in the first minute are four times more likely to require positive pressure ventilation, highlighting the importance of early heart rate assessment to guide resuscitation interventions.

pubmed.title: Procedural Techniques, Airway Management, and Specimen Acquisition for Peripheral Lung Transbronchial and Endobronchial Ultrasound-Guided Cryobiopsy: A Modified Delphi Consensus Statement.
pubmed.bottom_line: An international expert panel used a modified Delphi process to develop the first consensus statements on procedural techniques, airway management, and specimen handling for 1.1 mm cryoprobe transbronchial and endobronchial ultrasound-guided cryobiopsy outside of interstitial lung disease; while not based on direct clinical trial evidence, these expert-driven guidelines help standardize practice in a rapidly evolving area with significant procedural variability.

pubmed.title: Implementation of Double Sequential Defibrillation (DSD): An Aotearoa New Zealand observational study.
pubmed.bottom_line: This large retrospective study from New Zealand found no survival benefit after implementing double sequential defibrillation (DSD) for refractory VF/VT, with DSD patients showing lower odds of ROSC and 30-day survival—likely reflecting confounding by indication—highlighting that DSD should not be routinely adopted without further evidence.

pubmed.title: Prone Positioning in a North American Cohort of Hypoxemic Patients on Mechanical Ventilation.
pubmed.bottom_line: This large retrospective cohort study across 37 North American hospitals found that prone positioning for mechanically ventilated hypoxemic patients increased markedly during the COVID-19 pandemic but declined post-pandemic, with substantial inter-hospital variability; these findings highlight ongoing underuse and inconsistent implementation of a guideline-recommended, potentially life-saving intervention in ARDS.

pubmed.title: Understanding Patients with Mechanical Circulatory Support for Cardiogenic Shock: The Role of Preceding Cardiac Arrest.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this multicenter registry study, patients with infarct-related cardiogenic shock receiving mechanical circulatory support had similar 30-day mortality regardless of preceding out-of-hospital cardiac arrest (OHCA), but predictors of mortality differed between OHCA and non-OHCA groups; thus, clinical decisions should consider these distinct risk profiles rather than OHCA status alone.

pubmed.title: Post-dural Puncture Headache in Dural Puncture Epidural and Combined Spinal-Epidural Using 24- and 25-Gauge Needles Versus Conventional Epidural Labor Analgesia: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.
pubmed.bottom_line: This systematic review and meta-analysis of 16 RCTs (n=3278) found that the risk of post-dural puncture headache with dural puncture epidural or combined spinal-epidural using 24- or 25-gauge needles is very low and only slightly higher than with conventional epidural labor analgesia, suggesting that concerns about increased headache risk with these techniques and needle sizes may be overstated in clinical practice.

pubmed.title: A Modified Delphi Consensus-Based Comprehensive Checklist and Angoff Standard for Assessment of Competency in Brain Death/Death by Neurologic Criteria Determination.
pubmed.bottom_line: This expert consensus study developed a comprehensive, guideline-based checklist with critical actions and a minimum passing standard for assessing clinician competency in brain death/death by neurologic criteria determination, providing a standardized tool that can improve training and assessment in both adult and pediatric settings. The evidence is based on a rigorous Delphi and Angoff process involving leading experts, supporting its clinical relevance for ensuring accurate and consistent BD/DNC evaluations.

pubmed.title: Determinants of tidal recruitment/derecruitment assessed by electrical impedance tomography in spontaneously breathing ARDS patients.
pubmed.journal: American journal of respiratory and critical care medicine
pubmed.bottom_line: In this secondary analysis of 29 spontaneously breathing ARDS patients on pressure support ventilation, higher PEEP individualized by EIT reduced tidal recruitment/derecruitment (R/D) compared to standard PEEP, with R/D associated with lower PEEP, more negative transpulmonary pressures, higher respiratory drive/effort, and non-focal infiltrates; these findings suggest that optimizing PEEP using EIT may minimize injurious R/D in this population

pubmed.title: Preoperative Olanzapine and Quality of Recovery after Ambulatory Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this randomized, double-blind trial of 384 women undergoing ambulatory surgery, preoperative olanzapine 5 mg (in addition to standard antiemetics) significantly improved quality of recovery and reduced postdischarge nausea through postoperative day 2 compared to placebo, without prolonging PACU stay, supporting its use for enhanced postoperative recovery in this population.

pubmed.title: Clinical Decision Support to Reduce Opioid Prescribing at Discharge for Inpatients Undergoing Surgery (LESS Study): An Interrupted Time Series Analysis.
pubmed.bottom_line: A system-wide clinical decision support intervention significantly reduced both the amount and frequency of opioid prescriptions at discharge for surgical inpatients who had not recently received opioids, based on strong evidence from a large interrupted time series analysis; this suggests that such interventions can effectively decrease unnecessary opioid prescribing and potentially reduce opioid-related harms.

pubmed.title: Associations Between Time to Reduction and Complications in Patients With First-Time Total Hip Arthroplasty Dislocation: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
pubmed.bottom_line: This large retrospective cohort study found that delays in closed reduction after first-time total hip arthroplasty dislocation are associated with significantly longer post-reduction hospital stays, highlighting the clinical importance of prompt reduction to minimize hospitalization in this patient population.

pubmed.title: Ethical Complexities in Extracorporeal Life Support Management: Pearls From the New American Heart Association Ethics Guidelines.
pubmed.bottom_line: This article highlights key ethical considerations from the new American Heart Association guidelines for extracorporeal life support, emphasizing updated recommendations to guide clinicians in navigating complex decision-making; while not presenting new empirical evidence, it provides important, consensus-based guidance relevant to clinical practice.

pubmed.title: Delayed Awakening After Out-of-Hospital Cardiac Arrest: A Scoping Review of Definitions, Determinants, and Prognostic Implications.
pubmed.bottom_line: This scoping review found that delayed awakening after out-of-hospital cardiac arrest is common, largely influenced by treatment factors (such as sedation and therapeutic hypothermia), and does not rule out good neurological recovery, though most evidence is based on outdated practices; clinicians should avoid premature withdrawal of life-sustaining therapy in these patients.

pubmed.title: Effects of inflammatory phenotypes in acute respiratory distress syndrome on mortality and partitioning of lung and chest wall mechanics in patients in the USA and Canada: a retrospective cohort study.
pubmed.bottom_line: This large retrospective cohort study found that ARDS patients with a hyperinflammatory phenotype had significantly higher 60-day mortality and distinct lung and chest wall mechanics compared to hypoinflammatory patients, suggesting that inflammatory phenotype should be considered when tailoring lung-protective ventilation strategies for improved outcomes.

pubmed.title: Effects of the ABCDEF Bundle on Delirium, Function, and Quality of Life in Australian ICU Patients: A Pragmatic Randomized Controlled Trial.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this single-center randomized controlled trial, the ABCDEF bundle did not reduce delirium incidence or duration, nor improve functional status at ICU discharge compared to usual care, but did show a modest improvement in the "usual activities" quality of life domain at 90 days; these findings suggest limited impact of the bundle on primary clinical outcomes in this setting.

pubmed.title: Hydroxyethyl Starch and Perioperative Complications: a Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.
pubmed.bottom_line: This large, high-quality meta-analysis found that perioperative use of hydroxyethyl starch (HES 130/0.4 or 130/0.42) in surgical patients does not significantly increase the risk of acute kidney injury or worsen creatinine levels, suggesting that short-term HES use may be safer than previously thought, which is clinically relevant given recent regulatory restrictions.

pubmed.title: Timing of Onset of Persistent Critical Illness in Brazil: A Nested Secondary Analysis of the IMPACTO-MR Cohort Study.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this large multicenter Brazilian ICU cohort, persistent critical illness (PerCI) was defined as beginning on ICU day 15, when the predictive value of acute illness severity equaled or fell below that of antecedent patient characteristics; this finding provides a data-driven threshold for PerCI onset, which may inform clinical decision-making and resource allocation in critical care.

pubmed.title: Modifiable Risk Factors of Prolonged Grief Disorder, Posttraumatic Stress Disorder, Depression, and Anxiety Symptom States After ICU Loss.
pubmed.bottom_line: This prospective cohort study of bereaved ICU family members found that modifiable end-of-life care factors—such as having a do-not-resuscitate order and social worker involvement—were associated with lower risk of severe, co-occurring grief, PTSD, depression, and anxiety, highlighting the clinical importance of optimizing ICU end-of-life practices to reduce psychiatric morbidity in bereaved families.

pubmed.title: External Validation, Molecular Signatures, and Therapeutic Relevance of Pediatric Sepsis-Associated Acute Kidney Injury Subphenotypes.
pubmed.bottom_line: This large multicenter study externally validates two pediatric sepsis-associated acute kidney injury (SAKI) subphenotypes, showing that the pSAKI-2 group has significantly worse outcomes and distinct inflammatory biomarker profiles, and also identifies potential harm from corticosteroid use in pSAKI-1 patients; these findings support the clinical relevance of SAKI subphenotyping for risk stratification and personalized therapy in pediatric sepsis.

pubmed.title: Prevalence, Risk Factors, and Mortality of Venovenous Extracorporeal Membrane Oxygenation Escalation in Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome: A Multicenter Cohort Study From the Chinese Society of Extracorporeal Life Support Registry.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this large multicenter Chinese cohort of ARDS patients on venovenous ECMO, 4.7% required escalation to more advanced ECMO modes, which was independently associated with higher in-hospital mortality, especially in lower-volume centers; these findings highlight the importance of center experience and early identification of patients at risk for escalation.

pubmed.title: Costs and complications of extracorporeal membrane oxygenation therapy in Australia and New Zealand.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this large prospective registry study from Australia and New Zealand, ECMO therapy was associated with high costs—particularly for venovenous ECMO due to longer hospital stays—and complications, especially infections, substantially increased total patient costs; these findings highlight the importance of complication prevention and resource planning in ECMO management.

pubmed.title: The Effect of Readmission to the ICU on 60-Day Hospital Mortality in Patients With and Without Frailty: A Binational Registry-Based Study.
pubmed.bottom_line: This large registry-based study found that frail ICU patients are at higher risk for both ICU readmission and 60-day in-hospital mortality compared to non-frail patients, highlighting the need for targeted interventions and discharge planning in this vulnerable population.

pubmed.title: Does transthoracic impedance influence termination and recurrence of ventricular fibrillation during impedance-compensated shocks for Out-of-Hospital Cardiac Arrest? A retrospective study.
pubmed.bottom_line: This large retrospective study found that, even with impedance-compensated defibrillation, transthoracic impedance ≥100Ω is associated with reduced likelihood of terminating ventricular fibrillation/pulseless VT during out-of-hospital cardiac arrest, while impedance does not affect recurrence risk after successful termination; this suggests that high impedance remains a barrier to effective defibrillation despite compensation.

foamed.title: GLP-1 agonists and anesthesia risk: Assessment of gastric content using ultrasound
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: A new Mayo Clinic study of 316 adults on GLP-1 receptor agonists found these medications can delay gastric emptying, potentially increasing the risk of pulmonary aspiration during anesthesia, suggesting that standard preoperative fasting guidelines may not adequately mitigate this risk in patients taking GLP-1 RAs.

foamed.title: Essential Updates from POCUS Journals - JournalFeed
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: Recent studies highlighted in POCUS journals indicate that power Doppler POCUS accurately measures systolic blood pressure compared to intraarterial lines, ultrasound-guided PENG block provides superior analgesia over intravenous opioids in elderly hip fracture patients, and ultrasound-guided erector spinae plane block offers effective pain relief and reduces opioid use in pancreatic cancer patients.

foamed.title: HI-PEITHO: More negative data on invasive therapy for higher risk PEs
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: In the HI-PEITHO trial, ultrasound-facilitated, catheter-directed fibrinolysis combined with anticoagulation did not demonstrate a significant improvement in patient outcomes compared to anticoagulation alone in adults with intermediate-risk pulmonary embolism, adding to the growing body of negative data regarding invasive therapy for higher-risk PEs.

foamed.title: Extravasation injuries
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This educational article explains that extravasation injuries occur when intravenous fluids or medications leak into surrounding tissues, potentially causing complications ranging from mild swelling to tissue necrosis, and highlights the importance of early recognition and risk assessment in children due to their increased vulnerability; no new clinical data are presented.

foamed.title: Popliteal plexus block in TKA: A small gain or a meaningful step forward?
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article provides commentary and educational discussion on the potential role of popliteal plexus block (PPB) in total knee arthroplasty (TKA) pain management, referencing recent clinical trials but not presenting new primary data; robust evidence supporting routine PPB use remains limited at this time.

foamed.title: History of epidural needles and catheters
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article provides an educational overview of the historical development, design considerations, and clinical use of epidural needles and catheters, without presenting new clinical data or research findings.

foamed.title: Survival effect of prehospital emergency anaesthesia in major trauma
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article is an educational commentary discussing the ongoing controversy and limited evidence regarding whether prehospital emergency anaesthesia (PHEA) and tracheal intubation improve survival in major trauma, noting that existing studies are conflicting and no clear mortality benefit has been demonstrated in randomized trials to date.

foamed.title: EMCrit Wee – Compassion in Medicine – Compassionomics with Stephen Trzeciak
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: A Johns Hopkins randomized controlled trial found that a 40-second compassionate intervention—brief, empathetic statements from oncologists—significantly reduced anxiety in cancer patients during consultations, highlighting the measurable impact of compassion on patient well-being in clinical settings.

foamed.title: CT Case 107
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This educational case describes a 60-year-old male with a CT-confirmed hangman fracture (bilateral pars interarticularis fracture of C2) following a car accident, highlighting that such injuries account for 4–7% of cervical spine fractures and neurological impairment occurs in about 25% of cases, with atypical fractures posing greater neurological risk.

foamed.title: HI-PEITHO Trial: Ultrasound-facilitated, Catheter Directed Fibrinolysis for PE
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: The HI-PEITHO trial is the first RCT to show that ultrasound-facilitated, catheter-directed fibrinolysis plus anticoagulation reduces short-term hemodynamic decompensation compared to anticoagulation alone in highly selected intermediate-high risk PE patients, but these results are not broadly applicable to all intermediate-risk PE cases.

foamed.source_name: WFSA Anaesthesia Tutorial of the Week
foamed.title: Controversies in the perioperative management of the vulnerable brain
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article is an educational review discussing current controversies in the perioperative management of patients with vulnerable brains and does not present new clinical data or original research findings.

foamed.title: EM@3AM: Septic Abortion
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This emDocs EM@3AM article provides an educational clinical review of septic abortion, outlining its definition, presentation, and significance as a cause of pregnancy-related mortality, but does not present new research data or original study findings.

foamed.title: JournalFeed Blog | High-Yield Medical Research Summaries
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This narrative review and toxicological perspective found that intravenous contrast doses used for CT scans are well below the toxic threshold for nephrotoxicity, with multiple large studies showing no convincing evidence that such doses cause acute kidney injury, dialysis, or long-term renal harm in most patients.

foamed.title: Advances in Critical Care Medicine Insights - JournalFeed
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This narrative review concludes that intravenous contrast doses used for CT scans are well below nephrotoxic thresholds, with consistent evidence from large studies showing no convincing association between IV contrast for CT and acute kidney injury, dialysis, or long-term renal outcomes in most patients.

foamed.title: EMCrit Wee – How I Fracked Up at Incrementum 2026
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This EMCrit post is an educational reflection describing a personal clinical error during an awake intubation at Incrementum 2026, highlighting contributing technical and human factors, but it does not present new clinical data or research findings.

foamed.title: MDCalc Wars: sPESI vs Bova Score — PE Risk Stratification Made Practical
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This educational review compares the sPESI and Bova scores for pulmonary embolism (PE) risk stratification, emphasizing that sPESI is most useful for identifying low-risk patients suitable for discharge, while the Bova score helps detect normotensive patients at higher risk for early complications, but both tools should complement—not replace—clinical judgment.

foamed.title: Must-Read Emergency Department Articles Today - JournalFeed
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: Among adult emergency department boarders, longer boarding times and each additional hour waiting for an inpatient bed are associated with increased risk of early clinical deterioration, and patients who deteriorate during boarding experience significantly higher 28-day mortality, according to retrospective analysis.

foamed.title: Tenecteplase versus standard medical treatment for basilar artery occlusion within 24 h (TRACE-5): a multicentre, prospective, randomised, open-label, blinded-endpoint, superiority, phase 3 trial.
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: In the TRACE-5 trial, intravenous tenecteplase within 24 hours for confirmed basilar artery occlusion improved excellent functional outcomes at 90 days compared to standard care (38% vs. 29%, NNT ≈ 11) without increasing symptomatic hemorrhage or mortality, though findings are limited by single-ethnicity population and use of a biosimilar formulation.

foamed.title: TTL Tip 11: Be the TTL your prehospital teams want to hand over to
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article provides educational commentary on the importance of effective communication and preparation for handover by trauma team leaders (TTLs) in prehospital and emergency settings, emphasizing practical tips for optimizing team readiness and patient care during transitions, but does not present new clinical data or research findings.

foamed.title: Global EM 11 – Global Emergency Medicine Fellowships: More than Just Stamps in Your Passport
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article provides an educational overview of Global Emergency Medicine fellowships, describing their scope, potential career paths, and the author's personal experiences, but does not present new clinical research data or outcomes.

foamed.title: Diagnostic accuracy of the STANDING algorithm in patients with isolated vertigo: a multicentre prospective study (STANDING-M)
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: In this multicentre prospective study, the STANDING algorithm demonstrated high specificity (91.6%) and excellent rule-out ability (NPV 99%) for diagnosing isolated vertigo, reducing unnecessary imaging and emergency department length of stay compared to usual care, though limitations include few central vertigo cases and limited use of gold standard imaging.

foamed.title: TOP: Liberal or restrictive post-op transfusion
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This summary describes the TOP randomized clinical trial, which compared liberal (Hb <10 g/dL) versus restrictive (Hb <7 g/dL) postoperative transfusion thresholds in high cardiac risk adults after vascular or general surgery, but does not provide results or conclusions regarding the impact on 90-day mortality, ischemic events, or acute renal failure.

deep_dives.title: Post-dural Puncture Headache in Dural Puncture Epidural and Combined Spinal-Epidural Using 24- and 25-Gauge Needles Versus Conventional Epidural Labor Analgesia: A Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.
deep_dives.bottom_line: This systematic review and meta-analysis of 16 RCTs (n=3278) found that the risk of post-dural puncture headache with dural puncture epidural or combined spinal-epidural using 24- or 25-gauge needles is very low and only slightly higher than with conventional epidural labor analgesia, suggesting that concerns about increased headache risk with these techniques and needle sizes may be overstated in clinical practice.

deep_dives.title: Influence of Pre-emptive Haptoglobin on Postoperative Acute Kidney Injury in Cardiac Surgical Patients: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
deep_dives.bottom_line: In this small, single-center randomized controlled trial, pre-emptive haptoglobin therapy guided by serum-free hemoglobin did not significantly reduce postoperative acute kidney injury compared to standard care in cardiac surgery patients, and the study was stopped early due to safety concerns; thus, routine pre-emptive haptoglobin use for AKI prevention in this setting is not supported.

deep_dives.title: Preoperative Olanzapine and Quality of Recovery after Ambulatory Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial.
deep_dives.bottom_line: In this randomized, double-blind trial of 384 women undergoing ambulatory surgery, preoperative olanzapine 5 mg (in addition to standard antiemetics) significantly improved quality of recovery and reduced postdischarge nausea through postoperative day 2 compared to placebo, without prolonging PACU stay, supporting its use for enhanced postoperative recovery in this population.

pubmed.title: Development of a deep-learning model for classification of intensivists' extubation decisions using ventilator graphic monitor images: a prospective observational study.
pubmed.bottom_line: This prospective observational study developed a deep-learning model using ventilator graphic monitor images to classify intensivists' extubation decisions, with the best model (MobileNetV3-Large) achieving high accuracy (AUC 0.900) in a single-center cohort; this suggests AI could assist less experienced providers in making extubation decisions, though broader validation is needed.

foamed.title: Extravasation injuries
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This educational article reviews the causes, risk factors, identification, and potential complications of extravasation injuries in children, emphasizing the importance of early recognition and management but does not present new clinical data or research findings.

foamed.title: History of epidural needles and catheters
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article provides an educational overview of the historical development, design evolution, and functional considerations of epidural needles and catheters, without presenting new clinical data or research findings.

pubmed.title: Withholding or continuing glucose-lowering drugs for elective surgery in patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus: a secondary analysis of the MOPED international, prospective, observational study.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this large, prospective, observational study, continuing metformin (but not SGLT2 inhibitors or GLP-1 receptor agonists) on the day of elective surgery in patients with type 2 diabetes was associated with a small but statistically significant increase in days alive and at home at 30 days, suggesting potential benefit to not withholding metformin perioperatively.

pubmed.title: Prophylactic interventions to prevent rebound pain after peripheral nerve block in adults: a systematic review and network meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials.
pubmed.bottom_line: This systematic review and network meta-analysis of 24 RCTs (2130 patients) found that intravenous dexamethasone is the most effective intervention for preventing rebound pain after peripheral nerve block, with moderate certainty evidence, supporting its use as a first-line prophylactic agent in this setting.

deep_dives.title: Withholding or continuing glucose-lowering drugs for elective surgery in patients with type 2 diabetes mellitus: a secondary analysis of the MOPED international, prospective, observational study.
deep_dives.bottom_line: In this large, prospective, observational study, continuing metformin (but not SGLT2 inhibitors or GLP-1 receptor agonists) on the day of elective surgery in patients with type 2 diabetes was associated with a small but statistically significant increase in days alive and at home at 30 days, suggesting potential benefit to not withholding metformin perioperatively.

pubmed.title: Severe Preoperative Infection and Postoperative Acute Kidney Injury in Children: A Two-Hit Risk Factor for Mortality.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this large retrospective cohort study, children with severe preoperative infection had over twice the risk of postoperative acute kidney injury (AKI), and the combination of both conditions increased 30-day mortality risk ninefold, highlighting the need for heightened perioperative vigilance and preventive strategies in this high-risk pediatric population.

pubmed.title: The Prevalence of Fibromyalgia in the General Population and At-Risk Subpopulations: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
pubmed.bottom_line: This large systematic review and meta-analysis found that fibromyalgia affects about 1.4% of the general population worldwide (with very low certainty and high heterogeneity), but prevalence is much higher—up to 37%—in certain at-risk groups such as those with autoimmune or inflammatory diseases, highlighting the need for targeted screening and resource allocation in these populations.

pubmed.title: Decompression with or without Duraplasty for Chiari I and Syringomyelia.
pubmed.journal: The New England journal of medicine
pubmed.bottom_line: In this multicenter cluster-randomized trial in children with Chiari I malformation and syringomyelia, adding duraplasty to posterior fossa decompression did not significantly increase surgical complications but showed trends toward greater syrinx reduction and fewer repeat decompressions, though health-related quality of life was similar; larger studies are needed to clarify the clinical benefits and risks.

pubmed.title: Zanidatamab with and without Tislelizumab in HER2-Positive Gastroesophageal Cancer.
pubmed.journal: The New England journal of medicine
pubmed.bottom_line: In this phase 3 trial, zanidatamab (a bispecific HER2 antibody) plus chemotherapy—with or without tislelizumab—significantly improved progression-free survival compared to standard trastuzumab-chemotherapy in HER2-positive advanced gastroesophageal adenocarcinoma, and the addition of tislelizumab also significantly prolonged overall survival; these findings suggest a new, more effective first-line treatment option for this patient population.

foamed.title: ECG Pointers: MVC with Spinal Injury
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This article is an educational case-based discussion on ECG interpretation in the context of a motor vehicle collision with suspected spinal cord injury, and does not present new clinical data or research findings.

deep_dives.title: Decompression with or without Duraplasty for Chiari I and Syringomyelia.
deep_dives.journal: The New England journal of medicine
deep_dives.bottom_line: In this multicenter cluster-randomized trial in children with Chiari I malformation and syringomyelia, adding duraplasty to posterior fossa decompression did not significantly increase surgical complications but showed trends toward greater syrinx reduction and fewer repeat decompressions, though health-related quality of life was similar; larger studies are needed to clarify the clinical benefits and risks.

pubmed.title: Magnesium Sulfate to Prevent Perioperative Atrial Fibrillation in Cardiac Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial.
pubmed.bottom_line: This well-conducted randomized controlled trial found that perioperative magnesium sulfate infusion did not reduce the incidence of postoperative atrial fibrillation in cardiac surgery patients, indicating that routine prophylactic magnesium supplementation for POAF prevention is not supported.

pubmed.title: Reducing Physical Restraint in the ICU: Multicenter Phase II Randomized Trial of a Novel Device Versus Traditional Wrist Restraints.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this multicenter phase II randomized crossover trial of 54 ICU patients, a novel restraint device showed similar patient activity, safety, and satisfaction compared to traditional wrist restraints, suggesting it may be a viable alternative, though larger studies are needed to confirm these findings. This matters clinically as it offers a potential option for reducing physical restraint use in the ICU without compromising safety or patient experience.

pubmed.title: Traumatic Inflammatory-Coagulation Coactivation Occurs Early After Injury and Impacts Mortality.
pubmed.bottom_line: This secondary analysis of the PROPPR trial found that early coactivation of inflammatory and coagulation pathways after severe trauma is strongly associated with 30-day mortality, highlighting the clinical importance of promptly recognizing and targeting both inflammation and coagulopathy in trauma care.

pubmed.title: Interaction Between Admission Hemoglobin and Sex on ICU Mortality in Patients With Moderate-to-Severe Traumatic Brain Injury: A Retrospective Bicentric Cohort Study.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this large retrospective cohort study of moderate-to-severe TBI patients, admission hemoglobin was associated with ICU mortality, but there was no significant interaction between hemoglobin and sex, suggesting that sex-specific transfusion thresholds based solely on admission hemoglobin are not warranted; further prospective research is needed to confirm these findings.

pubmed.title: Potentially Surgical Digestive Complications in Patients With Status Epilepticus: Insights From the ICTAL Registry.
pubmed.bottom_line: In this large multicenter ICU cohort, potentially surgical digestive complications occurred in 1.6% of status epilepticus patients—most often in those with refractory SE and its treatments—and were independently associated with a threefold increase in hospital mortality, highlighting the need for vigilance and early recognition in this high-risk group.

pubmed.title: US Air Quality Index and respiratory health outcomes: background, knowledge gaps, and research prioritization.
pubmed.journal: American journal of respiratory and critical care medicine
pubmed.bottom_line: This research statement highlights significant gaps in evidence regarding the effectiveness of the US Air Quality Index (AQI) in improving respiratory health outcomes, especially among individuals with respiratory conditions, and prioritizes research to optimize AQI structure, communication, and clinical implementation; addressing these gaps is crucial for ensuring AQI guidance leads to meaningful health benefits.

pubmed.title: Phase 3 Results of Bepirovirsen Treatment for Chronic Hepatitis B Virus Infection.
pubmed.journal: The New England journal of medicine
pubmed.bottom_line: In two large phase 3 trials, bepirovirsen added to nucleos(t)ide analogue therapy achieved a functional cure (sustained HBsAg and HBV DNA loss) in about 20% of chronic hepatitis B patients versus none with placebo, but with higher rates of adverse events, supporting bepirovirsen as a promising new option for functional cure in this population.

foamed.title: Acute Liver Failure
foamed.bottom_line: BOTTOM LINE: This educational blog post reviews the definition, epidemiology, and clinical approach to acute liver failure, emphasizing its rarity, high morbidity and mortality, the importance of early recognition and referral, and clarifying diagnostic criteria, but does not present new clinical data or original research findings.

deep_dives.title: Magnesium Sulfate to Prevent Perioperative Atrial Fibrillation in Cardiac Surgery: A Randomized Clinical Trial.
deep_dives.bottom_line: This well-conducted randomized controlled trial found that perioperative magnesium sulfate infusion did not reduce the incidence of postoperative atrial fibrillation in cardiac surgery patients, indicating that routine prophylactic magnesium supplementation for POAF prevention is not supported.

deep_dives.title: Reducing Physical Restraint in the ICU: Multicenter Phase II Randomized Trial of a Novel Device Versus Traditional Wrist Restraints.
deep_dives.bottom_line: In this multicenter phase II randomized crossover trial of 54 ICU patients, a novel restraint device showed similar patient activity, safety, and satisfaction compared to traditional wrist restraints, suggesting it may be a viable alternative, though larger studies are needed to confirm these findings. This matters clinically as it offers a potential option for reducing physical restraint use in the ICU without compromising safety or patient experience.

deep_dives.title: Traumatic Inflammatory-Coagulation Coactivation Occurs Early After Injury and Impacts Mortality.
deep_dives.bottom_line: This secondary analysis of the PROPPR trial found that early coactivation of inflammatory and coagulation pathways after severe trauma is strongly associated with 30-day mortality, highlighting the clinical importance of promptly recognizing and targeting both inflammation and coagulopathy in trauma care.
